metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Grant Haschak (Ghash77)

set_overview.summary: Strixhaven is a unique Limited format centered on five colleges (two-color guilds). The set emphasizes instants/sorceries heavily — most decks contain 13+ spells. The Mystical Archive bonus sheet adds powerful historic spells (Lightning Bolt, Swords to Plowshares) that significantly impact draft strategy.
color_ranking: Tier 2: Silverquill Aggro (WB), Multicolor Good Stuff

color_notes.Note: Strixhaven is college-driven (2-color guilds). Each college supports a unique theme. Off-school decks emerge rarely — about once every 16 drafts.

key_gameplay_notes: Apply maximum pressure early — opponents may hold game-winning bombs.
Wait until pack 2 to lock into archetypes; flexibility wins.
Lesson cards are exceptionally valuable — Environmental Sciences is the best common in the set.
Mystical Archive includes 1-mana instants — expect cheap removal at instant speed.
Off-school splashes (e.g. black in Izzet) are difficult; multicolor fixing comes mainly from learn cards.

mechanics.name: Magecraft
mechanics.summary: Triggers whenever you cast or copy an instant or sorcery. Effects range from life gain to +1/+0 to card draw.
mechanics.performance: Highly effective in STX — most decks run 13+ spells.

mechanics.name: Learn / Lesson
mechanics.summary: Learn lets you search your sideboard for a lesson or discard and draw. Lessons function as flexible sideboard answers.
mechanics.performance: Format-defining. Environmental Sciences is the best common despite appearing weak in isolation.

key_examples.name: Environmental Sciences
key_examples.note: Best common in the set — fixes mana and ramps.

mechanics.name: Ward
mechanics.summary: Creatures with ward require opponents to pay a cost (mana or life) to target them.
mechanics.performance: Minor mechanic — only 4 cards in the set.

tier_1.name: Prismari (Izzet) — Expensive Spells & Copying
tier_1.strategy: Cast high-mana-cost spells and copy them for value. Tolerates the slow format better than other decks. Runs minimal creatures, leveraging instants/sorceries for removal and game-ending effects.

tier_1.name: Quandrix (Simic) — Lands Matter & Ramp
tier_1.strategy: Play 8+ lands to supercharge creatures. Ramp payoffs work independently. Vulnerable to aggressive decks with cheap removal.

tier_1.name: Temur Ramp (Off-Color)
tier_1.strategy: Combines Prismari and Quandrix best elements plus green acceleration. Punishes greed — multiple drafters fighting over it lead to bad decks.

tier_2.name: Silverquill Aggro (Orzhov)
tier_2.strategy: Evasive creatures + interaction + magecraft triggers. Runs closer to 13 spells, maximizing Silverquill Pledgemage. The one archetype that can consistently go under big mana decks. Cannot recover from stumbles.

tier_2.name: Multicolor Good Stuff
tier_2.strategy: Splash broken rares with Environmental Sciences mana fixing. Rarely comes together because Environmental Sciences is heavily drafted.

tier_3.name: Witherbloom (Golgari) — Lifegain Engine
tier_3.strategy: Gain life repeatedly to grow Blood Researcher. Critical flaw: a boosted Blood Researcher dies to common removal (Bury in Books, Divide by Zero) which bounces it back undoing growth.

tier_3.name: Lorehold (Boros) — Graveyard Value
tier_3.strategy: The designed graveyard theme underperforms. Better route: Lorehold Aggro with early 2-power creatures, Pledgemages, removal, and fliers — still struggles against big mana decks.

key_commons.name: Environmental Sciences
key_commons.note: Best common — lesson, fixes mana, ramps.

mystical_archive.description: Bonus sheet (sta) of historically powerful spells inserted into packs. 1-mana instants shift removal timing calculations.

biggest_trap.name: Mascot Exhibition
biggest_trap.note: Opponents learning for it often indicates misplays.

high_stakes.name: Professor Onyx
high_stakes.note: Binary outcome — you either navigate it or lose.

rich_get_richer.name: Mizzix's Mastery
rich_get_richer.note: Enables replaying your best spells indefinitely.

general_tips: Apply maximum pressure early — opponents may hold game-winning bombs.
Wait until pack 2 to commit to an archetype; flexibility yields better decks.
Lesson cards are premium even when they look weak — Environmental Sciences first-pick worthy.
Account for Mystical Archive 1-mana instants when calculating combat math.
Off-school splashes are hard; rely on Environmental Sciences for fixing.
Aggressive play beats conservative play in this format due to bomb density.
